author: Dr. Lizard
title: What causes elevated bilirubin despite improved hemoglobin on C5 inhibitors in PNH?
answer: Extravascular hemolysis from C3 fragment deposition on PNH red cells removed by splenic and hepatic macrophages.
detail: C5 inhibitors prevent intravascular hemolysis but allow ongoing proximal complement activation and C3 opsonization of red cells.
C3-coated erythrocytes are cleared by macrophages in spleen and liver, causing extravascular hemolysis without elevating LDH significantly.
Clinically significant extravascular hemolysis affects twenty to twenty-five percent of patients on ravulizumab or eculizumab therapy.
Direct antiglobulin test positive for C3d confirms C3 fragment deposition and extravascular hemolysis mechanism in these patients.
releaseNote: C5 inhibitors prevent intravascular hemolysis but allow ongoing extravascular hemolysis due to C3 fragment deposition on PNH red cells, affecting 20-25% of patients.
